title: Verbs Related to Architecture and Construction

sense.definition: to put together different materials such as brick to make a building, etc.
to make something by putting separate parts of something together
(of a construction) to fall down suddenly, particularly due to being damaged or weak
to cover with a mixture of cement, sand, gravel, and water

examples.x: The workers will concrete the driveway to ensure it lasts for years.
They decided to concrete the backyard for a more durable patio area.
The construction crew began to concrete the foundation of the new building early in the morning.
After the old pavement was removed, they were ready to concrete the area again.
To prevent erosion, they planned to concrete the riverbank.

sense.definition: to build a house, bridge, machine, etc.

examples.x: Engineers and construction workers collaborated to construct a sturdy and safe bridge.
The company is planning to construct a new office building to accommodate its growing workforce.
Skilled carpenters were hired to construct custom-made furniture for the luxury hotel.
Architects designed the blueprint, and construction crews worked together to construct the modern skyscraper.
To improve transportation, the city decided to construct a new subway system.

sense.definition: to completely destroy or to knock down a building or another structure

examples.x: The old factory was demolished to make way for a new development.
The wrecking ball was used to demolish the abandoned building.
The earthquake had the power to demolish poorly constructed houses.
The city decided to demolish the unsafe bridge for public safety.
The construction crew will demolish the existing walls before rebuilding.

sense.definition: to build or assemble a structure or object in an upright position

examples.x: The construction crew worked diligently to erect a new office building in the city center.
Engineers were tasked with erecting a sturdy bridge over the river to improve transportation.
To mark the historical site, the city decided to erect a monument in honor of the founding fathers.
The team successfully erected a large tent for the outdoor event, providing shelter for attendees.
The company planned to erect a solar power plant to harness clean energy for the community.

sense.definition: to dig a hole or make a channel in the ground

examples.x: The workers excavated a trench to lay down the foundation for the building.
Farmers excavated ditches to improve irrigation in the fields.
Archaeologists excavated test pits to assess the archaeological potential of the site.
Contractors excavated a hole for the septic tank installation in the yard.
The landscapers excavated a pond in the garden to create a water feature.

sense.definition: to insert a piece of glass into a building's window frame

examples.x: The carpenters glazed the windows of the new house with energy-efficient glass.
The renovation project involved glazing the storefront windows to create a more modern look for the shop.
The construction workers glazed the office building's windows with tempered glass for added safety and security.
The maintenance crew replaced the old, cracked glass and glazed the windows of the school.
The window installation team glazed the skylights with transparent glass to maximize natural light in the atrium.

sense.definition: to completely destroy the inside of a room, building, or vehicle, particularly by fire
to destroy a structure such as building or wall

examples.x: The bulldozers are knocking down the old buildings to make way for a new development.
The construction workers are knocking down the wall to create an open-plan living space.
The authorities plan to knock down the condemned building to prevent it from collapsing.

sense.definition: to destroy a building, area, etc. completely

examples.x: The demolition crew leveled the old factory to make way for the new development project.
The hurricane leveled entire neighborhoods, leaving behind a path of destruction.
The controlled explosion leveled the condemned building, clearing the space for urban renewal.
Wildfires swept through the forest, leveling acres of trees and wildlife habitats.
The bombing raid leveled residential areas, leaving civilians displaced and homeless.

sense.definition: to cover a wall, ceiling, etc. with a soft usually white material that is made of sand, water, and lime in order to smooth its surface

examples.x: The artisan plastered the walls of the medieval castle to repair cracks and imperfections.
We hired a contractor to plaster the newly constructed addition to our home.
He meticulously plastered the ceiling of the living room to create a smooth and uniform surface.
The craftsman plastered the archways in the hallway to enhance the architectural details.
The workshop participants learned how to plaster the walls using traditional methods.

sense.definition: to demolish a structure or building, typically by pulling it apart or taking it down piece by piece

examples.x: Over the next month, the workers will pull down the unsafe structures in the area.

sense.definition: to build something once again, after it has been destroyed or severely damaged

examples.x: After the hurricane, the town worked hard to rebuild the damaged homes.
The organization aims to rebuild communities affected by natural disasters.
The city plans to rebuild the old bridge using modern materials.
The school district received funding to rebuild the playground.
The architect was hired to rebuild the historic site according to its original design.

sense.definition: to make or build something once again after it has been destroyed or damaged

examples.x: After the earthquake, they had to reconstruct their house from the ground up.
The archaeologists worked diligently to reconstruct the ancient ruins.
The city decided to reconstruct the bridge to improve traffic flow.
He is reconstructing the model airplane in his workshop.
We have reconstructed the damaged road to improve safety.

sense.definition: to make a room or building look more attractive by repairing, redecorating, or cleaning it

examples.x: They refurbished the old house to make it livable again.
The hotel was refurbished with modern furniture and decor.
He refurbished the antique table to restore its original beauty.
The company refurbishes used laptops before selling them.
The museum was refurbished to attract more visitors.

sense.definition: to strengthen a substance or structure, particularly by adding extra material to it

examples.x: The builder decided to reinforce the foundation of the house by adding additional support beams.
To withstand heavy use, the backpack was reinforced with extra stitching and durable fabric.
The chef chose to reinforce the flavor of the soup by adding a rich and concentrated broth.
Engineers reinforced the bridge with steel cables to ensure it could bear heavier loads.
In preparation for the storm, residents reinforced their windows with protective shutters.

sense.definition: to cover the surface of a wall with plaster or cement
to replace or install new electrical wiring in something, like a building or a machine

examples.x: The old house was unsafe, so the electricians had to completely rewire it.
The technicians were hired to rewire the factory's outdated machinery.
He decided to rewire the lighting system in the living room for better brightness.
After the renovation, the contractor suggested rewiring the entire office for modern needs.
The mechanic had to rewire parts of the car to fix the electrical issue.

sense.definition: to connect or provide with wires or electrical cables
to rub a surface with sandpaper or another abrasive material to smooth, shape, or remove imperfections

examples.x: The carpenter sanded the wooden furniture to prepare it for staining.
She carefully sanded the rough edges of the DIY project to ensure a polished finish.
The artist sanded the canvas before applying layers of paint.
The homeowner sanded the old paint off the exterior walls for a fresh look.
The woodworker sanded the floorboards to remove scratches and blemishes.

sense.definition: to hold a person or thing in position or prevent them from falling

examples.x: The scaffolding was strategically placed to support the workers as they painted the exterior of the building.
The strong branches of the oak tree supported the tire swing, allowing children to play safely in the backyard.
Railings along the staircase are designed to support individuals and prevent accidental falls.
The safety harness was securely fastened to support the rock climber as they ascended the steep cliff.

sense.definition: to cover the roof of a house with a material made of reeds or straws
to cover the surface of a wall, floor, etc. with flat and often square pieces of baked clay or other materials
to make it so that nothing can move through something

examples.x: After the heavy rain, leaves and debris tended to clog the gutters.
The excess hair tends to clog the shower drain over time.
In a preventive measure, they decided to clog the air vents during the construction work.
A buildup of dirt and grease clogged the kitchen pipes, leading to a plumbing problem.
A swarm of insects clogged the air filter of the HVAC system, affecting air quality in the building.

sense.definition: to measure or determine the verticality or alignment of something, typically using a plumb bob or level
to set or install something in place, such as laying bricks or tiles

examples.x: He spent the weekend laying new carpet in the living room.
The mason will lay the bricks carefully to ensure a sturdy wall.
It took several days to lay the intricate mosaic tiles in the kitchen.
She hired a contractor to lay hardwood flooring throughout the house.
The tile installer will lay the ceramic tiles in a herringbone pattern for added visual interest.

sense.definition: to strike repeatedly and forcefully with a blunt object or tool such as a hammer
to set a piece of equipment in place and make it ready for use

examples.x: To upgrade the kitchen, they hired a professional to install the new dishwasher.
The electrician was called to install the lighting fixtures in the newly constructed room.
Homeowners often hire professionals to install air conditioning units for proper cooling.
The mechanic will install the new engine components to improve the car's performance.
To enhance energy efficiency, they decided to install solar panels on the roof.

sense.definition: to put decorative pieces of wood or metal into the surface of an object in a way that they level with the surface

examples.x: The artisan carefully inlaid precious stones into the wooden jewelry box for an elegant touch.
The furniture maker decided to inlay a contrasting wood to create a beautiful mosaic effect on the tabletop.
To enhance the guitar's appearance, the luthier inlaid intricate patterns along the fretboard.
The mosaic artist skillfully inlaid colored tiles to form a detailed picture on the floor.
The interior designer recommended inlaying metal accents into the kitchen backsplash for a modern look.

sense.definition: to keep a vehicle, building, road, etc. in good condition by doing regular repairs, renovations, or examinations

examples.x: The company hires professionals to maintain the office building each year.
It’s important to regularly maintain your car to avoid costly repairs down the road.
The city allocates funds to maintain public parks and recreational areas.
They maintain the old house by repainting and repairing it every few years.
The hotel maintains its facilities well, ensuring guests have a pleasant experience.

sense.definition: to make drawings according to which something will be constructed or produced

examples.x: The architect often designs modern homes with sustainable features.
They designed a prototype that revolutionized the industry.
By the time the project was due, they had already designed the entire product.
She has recently designed a series of fashion sketches.

sense.definition: to design, build, or plan something systematically and skillfully, especially using scientific principles and technical knowledge

examples.x: Scientists and engineers collaborated to engineer a solution to the complex environmental issue.
The company sought to engineer a cutting-edge software system to streamline operations.
The company is currently engineering a new software platform to meet evolving user needs.
The team skillfully engineered a solution to the complex problem.

sense.definition: to join two or more pieces of metal together using heat and pressure

examples.x: He carefully welded the steel beams to create a sturdy frame.
The blacksmith skillfully welded the broken iron gate back into place.
The automotive technician had to weld the exhaust pipe to fix the leak.
During the construction, workers needed to weld the joints of the pipeline for durability.
The engineer decided to weld the metal brackets to ensure a secure attachment.

sense.definition: to create or build something by combining different parts or components, either artificial or natural

examples.x: Car manufacturers fabricate vehicles from thousands of individual components.
Using the scattered resources on the island, the stranded sailors tried to fabricate a raft.
The engineer was tasked to fabricate a new prototype using the provided materials.
The blacksmith skillfully fabricated a custom iron gate for the entrance.
In the lab, scientists fabricate artificial organs for medical research and transplantation.

sense.definition: to take apart or disassemble a structure, machine, or object, breaking it down into its individual parts

examples.x: The mechanic had to dismantle the engine to identify and fix the issue.
They decided to dismantle the old playground equipment and replace it with new structures.
In order to recycle the materials, they needed to dismantle the outdated electronics.
The construction crew had to dismantle the scaffolding once the building was complete.
The scientists carefully dismantled the experimental setup to analyze the individual components.

sense.definition: to design or create a blueprint, layout, or diagram for a project or undertaking

examples.x: The architect plans the building by drafting detailed blueprints.
Before starting the project, they carefully planned the layout to ensure efficiency.
The engineer plans the bridge's design using complex diagrams and calculations.
He planned the new office space to maximize the use of available space.
She planned the remodel of the house, including a detailed floor plan.

sense.definition: to carefully examine something to check its condition or make sure it meets standards

examples.x: The mechanic will inspect the car to ensure it is safe to drive.
The teacher will inspect the students' work to ensure they have followed instructions.
The building inspector will inspect the construction site to verify compliance with safety standards.
The quality control team inspects the products before they are shipped to customers.
The supervisor inspects the machinery to detect any signs of wear or malfunction.

sense.definition: to construct or create the basic structure or framework of something, such as a building, by assembling its supporting elements

examples.x: The construction crew framed the house by erecting the wooden beams and structural supports.
Carpenters framed the new office building using steel beams and concrete pillars.
The workers framed the walls of the barn with sturdy timber and secured them in place.
The contractors framed the roof of the garage with trusses and plywood sheathing.
The team framed the deck of the beach house with pressure-treated lumber and stainless steel screws.

sense.definition: to design or modify the features and layout of an outdoor area, such as a garden, park, or public space, for aesthetic or functional purposes
to fasten or lock something firmly in place to prevent movement, damage, or unauthorized access

examples.x: Sarah secured her bicycle to the rack with a sturdy lock before going into the store.
The carpenter secured the shelves to the wall to ensure they wouldn't fall.
He secured the ladder to the scaffolding with safety straps before climbing up.
They secured the cargo to the truck bed with heavy-duty straps for transportation.

sense.definition: to close, fasten, or secure something tightly or airtight using a sealant, adhesive, or other means to prevent the passage of air, water, or other substances

examples.x: Before shipping, they had to seal the package with tape.
Frustrated with the leak, they needed to seal the crack in the pipe.
In preparation for the winter, they decided to seal the gaps around the windows.
The team is sealing the packages for shipping.
